steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence describes a method that sounds crude — a cardboard cutout and a piece of string. The second sentence reveals it worked surprisingly well. The blank must signal a contrast: something unexpected follows despite what came before.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Still,: Still is a contrast transition meaning "nevertheless" or "even so." It signals that despite the crude method, an accurate result was obtained — exactly the relationship the passage needs. ✓
  • Choice B — Similarly,: Similarly signals that what follows is comparable to what came before. That would imply the second idea is like the first, but the passage is contrasting a crude method with a surprisingly accurate result, not equating two similar ideas. ✗
  • Choice C — In other words,: In other words signals a restatement or clarification of the same idea. The second sentence is not restating the first — it introduces a new, contrasting outcome. ✗
  • Choice D — Fittingly,: Fittingly implies the result is appropriate or expected given what came before. But the whole point is that accuracy from such a crude method is surprising, not expected. ✗
A is correct.
